scope: Primary focus is newer sub-1B SLMs, especially 2025-2026 releases. Older models are only optional baselines.
selection_rule: Include only text-generative models with parameter_count_b < 1.0 as primary models. Models at exactly 1B or above are excluded from the main analysis.

primary_models.why_include: Very recent 230M edge model designed for agentic tasks, tool use, and data extraction; directly aligned with schema-abstention extraction tests.
primary_models.expected_engine: transformers, vLLM, llama.cpp via GGUF variant

primary_models.why_include: Recent 350M on-device model with strong edge inference claims; useful for comparing 230M vs 350M behavior.
primary_models.expected_engine: transformers, vLLM, llama.cpp via GGUF variant

primary_models.why_include: Newer Qwen sub-1B model; useful strong upper-end sub-1B comparison with long-context and instruction-following behavior.
primary_models.expected_engine: transformers, vLLM, llama.cpp via third-party GGUF if available
primary_models.gguf_or_quantized_variant: Use a pinned Qwen3.5-0.8B GGUF/MLX conversion if raw HF model is too heavy for local hardware.

primary_models.why_include: Very small 100M instruction-tuned model from the Falcon-H1-Tiny series; tests whether schema pressure appears at extremely tiny scale.
primary_models.expected_engine: transformers, vLLM, SGLang, Docker model runner, GGUF conversion if available
primary_models.gguf_or_quantized_variant: Community GGUF variants may exist; pin exact source if used.

primary_models.why_include: Recent 600M tiny reasoning model; tests whether reasoning-oriented training improves or worsens abstention under schemas.
primary_models.expected_engine: transformers, vLLM, SGLang, llama.cpp via GGUF variant

primary_models.why_include: Recent IBM Granite Nano instruct model for on-device and research use; useful enterprise/open baseline.
primary_models.expected_engine: transformers, vLLM, llama.cpp via GGUF variant

primary_models.why_include: Hybrid Mamba/Transformer-style Granite Nano model; tests whether hybrid architecture changes schema-abstention behavior.
primary_models.expected_engine: transformers, vLLM, llama.cpp via GGUF variant

primary_models.why_include: Function-calling model based on Gemma 3 270M; directly relevant to JSON/tool-call style structured output.
primary_models.expected_engine: transformers or Gemma-compatible local runtime; license/access may require authentication.
primary_models.gguf_or_quantized_variant: Check official or community quantized variants; pin exact revision.

primary_models.why_include: Recent extraction-tuned 350M model designed to output structured formats including JSON, XML, and YAML; ideal stress test for schema-induced hallucination.
primary_models.expected_engine: transformers, vLLM, llama.cpp via GGUF variant

optional_recent_backup_models.why_include: Compact 270M model for task-specific fine-tuning and text structuring; useful fallback if FunctionGemma access fails.
Older than Qwen3.5 but still a useful continuity baseline under 1B.

excluded_from_primary.reason: Older baseline; only use if the paper needs a historical comparison.
Older baseline; not part of new-primary model list.
Above the sub-1B threshold and older.
At/above 1B, outside the strict sub-1B scope.
